comment.body:
Tried my best, prof. Getting some clarity, but still a lot to study and put the picture together. But these excercises are very helpful, especially with you following up later:) 

ATM: Zomma effect is negative, and steeper for high vol suppression(-40% or more suppression) otherwise flatter. So both vol up and vol down,gamma decreases, but much more for vol down, except end of week where zomma effect flips positive for high vol supression, and gamma increases. Apart from this, across time axis zomma effect is relatively flat. Conclusion: Zomma effect is present both on vol down and vol up moves and decreases gamma, starker decrease for vol suppression.

So vol up, gamma down. Vol down, gamma down even stronger.
So in both cases, vol expands (since gamma is inverse to vol.). So if call side catches a steady bid (vol down) zomma effect is going to drop gamma fast and increase vol and then zomma effects is less strong but still reducing gamma so vol increases, but less strongly than at surpression levels, so spot gets downside pressure both if it gets bid up steadily (stronger here) as well as when vol increasws, and it starts dropping.

Vomma effect is also negative, relatively flat on the vol change axis, but very steep on the time axis going to 0 vomma effect, towards end of week for suppression, and earlier for vol expansion. So for vol expansion strong vomma effect beginning of the week, and only half as strong for vol suppression, but still all vega suppressing. Conclusion: Negative vomma effect across all days, and both for vol compression and suppression, but stronger for vol expansion early in the week, and fading faster into week end for vol expansion than suppression.
So volup, vega down. Vol down, vega down but less strong. Soin both vol up and vol down, vega reduces for MM, so upward pressure on IV. Hedges more expensive. (Still unclear on spot effect here??)

Tail:
Zomma effect positive, steep profile, both across vol change and time axis(less steep here). For vol up high zomma effect, especially beginning of the week, positive zomma effect fades into week end steeply.
Almost flat 0 zomma effect for vol down into week end, more especially for high vol suppression, but for less strong vol suppression and earlier in the week steep and positive zomma effect.
Conclusion: zomma effect more focused on vol up side, but also present on voldown (and steeply).

So vol up, gamma up. Vol down, gamma up (but less strongly than vol up side).So if downside momentum accelerates, dips get bought, and vol gets surpressed, slowing downside moves, swallowing vol. But this effect decades over the week. So downside gets protected strongly early in the week and more downside risk later in the week.
Vomma effect positive, similar to zomma. Steep across vol up side, and steep across time axis but focus on vol up side. and almost flat across time, except for vol suppression at beginning of week. So vol up, vega up steeply. Vol down, small vega up effect, beginning of week. So if vol up, MM vega exposure increases steeply, so there is downward pressure on IV. (Here im still struggling how that impacts spot, or if this is all about smirk convexity and kurtosis that you mentioned during the week…so got some more studying to do. Does this just mean hedging unwinds?)

comment.body:
heres my shot quant forgive my mistakes

vomma...i took it as....if IV continues to rise next week around your 6531 level then sensitivity of that IV which is vega should peak somewhere between there and 6412 so the tape becomes supportive in these bottom ranges.....the tail puts seem to have high positive vomma early in the week which could support a vol spike...the second image about zomma affect on ATM options which look to show negative vomma early in the week suggesting the ATM options do not get the same boost the tail puts get due to difference in high positive vomma on the tailputs vs the difference in negative vomma ATM. 

zomma of the tailput is also VERY positive vs the flatter ATM zomma which again showcasing tail puts convexity vs the ATM

comment.body:
I'm also coming back more refined. Just saw the levels thing. If is understand the levels to the 3d surfaces correctly. Something like this, looks similar to this week, stealing your vocab here Almita, which is starting to make a lot more sense now...

(6416.73 vomma positive TAIL
6531.38 vomma negative ATM

6416.85 zomma positive TAIL
6557.18 zomma negative ATM)

6557 negative zomma ATM all around, vol up, gamma down, and vol down gamma down even stronger (at least early in the week). Flip to vol down, gamma up later in the week on vol suppress side.
So either way gamma down early in the week, spot has downside pressure at this level, from zomma effect.
6531.38 negative vomma, almost flat across vol changes, decays into week end. Vol up and vol down, Vega down. MM becomes shorter Vega, so IV is increased, reflexively increasing RV? So either way vol goes up, we get again downside pressure
6416.85 steep positive zomma across IV and time, so the earlier we come to this zone in the week and the quicker (high vol) the more supported the zone is, since pos zomma means gamma up, vol down aka means MM buys dips and sells rips, supportive, aka synthetic circuit breaker again? Vol down side flattening into week end. Positive zomma centered around spot for vol up side.
6416.73 vomma positive, steeper than zomma , so earlier in the week and for high vol more supportive. Vol up/Vega up, MM sells IV, reflexively compresses RV? Again supportive zone here earlier in the week for vol up.

So early in the week supportive, should stay above your vomma model downside zone (Mon., Tuesday), I'm interpreting/guessing? But if we come to this zone later in the week, no supportive zomma and vomma effect left, could drop more vigorously on fr. again?
